name: Printer ink subscription entanglement
summary: Printers and similar appliances locked to proprietary consumable subscriptions.

evidence.claim: HP Instant Ink and similar printer subscription services use firmware locks to prevent non-subscription cartridges from being used in subscription-enrolled printers. Multiple class-action lawsuits (2023-2024) alleged this violates Magnuson-Moss tie-in prohibitions.
Magnuson-Moss 16 CFR 700.10 prohibits conditioning warranty coverage on use of branded parts unless the manufacturer provides those parts free.

body.description: An appliance that functions as designed only when paired with a manufacturer's proprietary consumable subscription. Firmware locks, DRM'd cartridges, or restricted warranty terms make third-party alternatives impossible or risky. Potential Magnuson-Moss tie-in violation (E2).

disclosureLegality.requirement: Warranty cannot be conditioned on proprietary-consumable use unless manufacturer provides free (E2).

negotiability.script: Please confirm in writing that use of third-party compatible cartridges will not void my warranty. Under the Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act, you cannot condition warranty coverage on the use of branded consumables unless you provide them free.
